description_long: This is a vintage mechanical cash register with a robust, rectangular metal body painted in a worn beige color, showing signs of age and use. The front features a prominent numeric display window at the top, a metallic lever on the right, and a grid of round, raised buttons in various shades, likely for entering amounts and functions. Below the keypad, a large black cash drawer is integrated into the base, with a slightly protruding lip for easy access. The sides are plain, with the left and right profiles revealing the stepped structure of the register and the depth of the cash drawer. The back is flat and unadorned, with visible wear along the edges. The overall design suggests durability and functionality, typical of mid-20th-century cash registers found in urban shops and markets.
description: A vintage beige metal cash register with a numeric display, round buttons, and a large black cash drawer, showing visible wear and age.
description_view_0: Left side view showing the stepped profile and the depth of the cash drawer, with no visible buttons or display.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the numeric display, round keypad, metallic lever, and the full width of the cash drawer.
description_view_2: Right side view similar to the left, highlighting the stepped structure and the cash drawer's extension.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a flat, plain beige surface with worn edges and no interactive features.
attribute_keypad_layout: numeric grid with approximately 20 round keys, arranged in rows and columns, some with colored labels
